title: RabbitMQ Queue Overflow Causing Message Loss
symptoms: RabbitMQ queue depth exceeding 4GB memory limit
Flow control activated on publisher connections
Disk space alarm triggered on RabbitMQ nodes
Publisher application not handling flow control signals
root_cause: Publisher service did not implement RabbitMQ backpressure handling, continuing to publish messages during flow control, leading to message drops when both memory and disk limits were breached
resolution: Cleared the overflowed queue, implemented publisher-side backpressure handling with confirms, increased disk space allocation, and resent lost notifications from application event log
preventive_actions: Implement mandatory publisher confirms and backpressure handling
Add queue depth monitoring with alerts at 1GB and 3GB
Configure dead letter queues for overflow scenarios
Implement message persistence for critical notification events
Create disaster recovery playbook for message loss scenarios

timeline.event: RabbitMQ flow control activated, publishers throttled
Alert fires for queue depth exceeding threshold
Mike Torres investigates, identifies publisher ignoring flow control
Disk space alarm triggers, messages begin being dropped
Queue cleared, publisher hotfix deployed with backpressure handling
Queue processing normalized, lost message audit initiated
34,000 lost notifications resent from application event log

embedding_summary: RabbitMQ queue overflow on notification-events queue due to publisher not implementing backpressure handling. 34,000 notification messages permanently lost when both memory and disk limits breached. Resolved by implementing publisher confirms, clearing queue, and resending lost notifications.
